listings.title: 24 Jolma Road
listings.locationDescription: Just off the Mass Turnpike and Route 12
listings.listingSummary:
45,300 SF Industrial Building on 2.87 AC for sale
Back on Market
LOCATION  HIGHLIGHTS
Strategically located in Worcester just off the Mass
Turnpike and Route 146
• 1.1 Miles to Route 20
• 2.4 Miles to I-90
• 3.6 Miles to Route 146

PROPERTY SPECIFICATIONS
Building Size: 45,300 SF
Manufacturing/Office: 29,000 SF
Warehouse: 16,000 SF
Lot Size: 2.87 AC
Clear Height:  14' - 16'
Loading:
Six (6) tailboard
with levelers; two
(2) drive-in doors
Heat: Gas
Power:
3000 amps 
480v/277w 
3 phase, 4 wire
Construction: Block
Roof: Rubber membrane
Air Conditioning: Office/Manufacturing
Zoning: BG-2

outreachTargets.emailSubject: Strategic Leasing Opportunity for Kamco Supply Corp.
outreachTargets.emailBody:
Dear Kamco Operations Team,

I hope this message finds you well. I am reaching out to share a promising leasing opportunity that could be a significant asset to Kamco Supply Corp.'s operations in Worcester.

The property at 24 Jolma Road, Worcester, offers a 45,300 SF industrial facility, strategically located for optimal distribution access near I-290/I-90. Given its proximity to your current location, just 4.8 miles away, this site could serve as an operationally superior central node, enhancing your access and distribution capacity across Central MA.

We believe this facility aligns well with your needs for warehouse access, efficient layout, and truck functionality. Additionally, the 10-year lease term provides the term certainty desired, with owner flexibility to consider phased occupancy and modest tenant improvements to minimize relocation downtime.

We would love to discuss how this opportunity can fit into your strategic operations. Could we schedule a quick call this week to explore this further?

Looking forward to your thoughts.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Contact Information]
[Your Company Name]

analysis.propertySnapshot: Known facts: 45,300 SF industrial building on 2.87 acres in Worcester, MA, just off I-90/Route 12/146. Mix of 29,000 SF manufacturing-office and 16,000 SF warehouse. Clear height 14-16 feet. Six tailboard docks with levelers and two drive-ins. Gas heat. 3,000-amp 480/277V 3-phase power. Block construction, rubber membrane roof, AC in office/manufacturing. Zoning listed as BG-2. Key assumption: BG-2 allows some industrial/flex/commercial uses but may limit heavier manufacturing, outdoor storage, and residential conversion without relief.
analysis.developerSummary: Good infill flex-industrial basis candidate if pricing reflects functional obsolescence. Best uses are owner-user manufacturing/flex, multi-tenant small-bay industrial, or limited industrial outdoor-storage-light reposition if zoning cooperates. Low clear height and tailboard loading cap top-tier warehouse rents, so underwrite as service industrial/flex, not modern logistics. Residential or large-scale redevelopment is not the first move here.
redFlags: Need zoning confirmation on exact BG-2 use table, parking, outdoor storage, and subdivision/tenanting limits
14-16 foot clear is below modern warehouse standard and narrows tenant pool
Tailboard loading is functional but inferior to full dock-high for many users
Unknown environmental history for manufacturing use; Phase I likely non-optional
Unknown roof age, floor slab condition, fire suppression, and deferred maintenance
2.87-acre coverage may constrain trailer storage, circulation upgrades, and expansion

scenarios.name: Owner-user flex/manufacturing acquisition with light modernization
scenarios.whyItFits: Existing power, mixed office-manufacturing layout, and highway access suit regional manufacturer, fabricator, food-adjacent processor, lab-support, or trade contractor HQ needing immediate functionality more than modern bulk specs.
whatMustBeTrue: BG-2 permits intended industrial/flex use as-of-right or by straightforward permit
Building systems, slab, and roof are fundamentally serviceable
Parking and truck circulation meet user needs without major site work
Acquisition basis is below replacement cost by a wide margin
scenarios.operatorSkillRequired: Low to medium; standard industrial leasing/construction management
scenarios.exitFlipability: Good to owner-user or private investor if basis stays sensible
scenarios.timeline: 4-9 months to diligence, close, and complete light capex
scenarios.targetTenantOrBuyer: Owner-user manufacturer, trade contractor, food/non-food processor, industrial service business
scenarios.revenueModel: Save occupancy cost or lease excess area; modest mark-to-market via cosmetic and systems upgrades
scenarios.exitStrategy: Long-term hold or resale to owner-user/private industrial investor
buildOutScope: LED lighting and basic power distribution cleanup
incentives: Possible local/state job creation or manufacturing equipment programs
killPoints: Environmental contamination with material cleanup cost
Use not permitted under BG-2 without risky relief
Seller pricing it like modern warehouse product

scenarios.name: Multi-tenant small-bay industrial/flex reposition
scenarios.whyItFits: Worcester supports demand from smaller industrial users. The current mix of office/manufacturing and warehouse can be demised for 5,000-15,000 SF users who value power and location over high clear heights.
whatMustBeTrue: Demising is physically practical with separate utilities/access
Parking count and code egress work after subdivision
Local leasing comps support enough rent spread over capex
scenarios.operatorSkillRequired: Medium; requires leasing, tenant coordination, and capex control
scenarios.exitFlipability: Good if stabilized with diversified rent roll
scenarios.targetTenantOrBuyer: Small manufacturers, assembly, medical/service industrial, contractors, distributors, showroom-flex users
scenarios.revenueModel: Create multiple suites and raise blended rent through smaller-bay premium
scenarios.exitStrategy: Refi after stabilization or sell to industrial syndicator/private buyer
buildOutScope: Restroom and office upgrades by suite
Parking/loading striping and site circulation improvements
incentives: Potential local tax dialogue if job creation is material
killPoints: Need for full sprinkler/fire alarm overhaul at high cost
Weak small-bay rent comps versus total basis

scenarios.name: Service industrial with limited outside storage or contractor yard
scenarios.whyItFits: Site size and drive-in loading may appeal to plumbing/HVAC/electrical, building materials, municipal vendor, or equipment service users if zoning permits controlled outdoor storage. This plays to function over image.
whatMustBeTrue: BG-2 expressly permits or can readily permit contractor yard/service industrial use
Site can support fenced outdoor area without crippling parking/fire access
Environmental and stormwater constraints are manageable
scenarios.exitFlipability: Fair; buyer pool narrower but sticky if permitted
scenarios.targetTenantOrBuyer: Contractor HQ, equipment service company, building products distributor, utility-related operator
scenarios.revenueModel: Single-tenant lease premium if legal outside storage is secured
scenarios.exitStrategy: Net lease or modified gross hold, then sell on stabilized cash flow
buildOutScope: Office refresh and dispatch area upgrades
incentives: Potential utility/security rebates only; no major subsidy case
killPoints: Outdoor storage prohibited or politically difficult
Truck circulation inadequate for target user
Site coverage leaves no functional yard after parking/code requirements
